zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Berwick Milton於1570年出生在英國奧可福爾斯坦頓 聖 約翰時，他的父親Richard Edison Melton II34歲，母親Elizabeth Houghton35歲。他於1589年8月5日與Francisca Tayler在英國英格蘭越可爾澤特林頓結婚。他去世於1640年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州加斯托享年70歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州加斯托。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Berwick Milton于1570年出生在英国奧可福爾斯坦頓 聖 約翰时，他的父亲Richard Edison Melton II34岁，母亲Elizabeth Houghton35岁。他于1589年8月5日与Francisca Tayler在英国英格兰越可爾澤特林頓结婚。他去世于1640年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州加斯托享年70岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州加斯托。

nameMeanings.name: Milton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the many places called Milton, or sometimes Middleton (see Middleton ) and occasionally Melton (see Melton ), most of which derive from Old English middel ‘middle’ or myln ‘mill’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Middleton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places so called. In over thirty instances from many different areas, the name is from Old English midel ‘middle’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. However, Middleton on the Hill near Leominster in Herefordshire appears in Domesday Book as Miceltune, the first element clearly being Old English micel ‘large’, ‘great’ (see Middlebrook ). Middleton Baggot and Middleton Priors in Shropshire have early spellings that suggest gem̄thhyll (from gem̄th ‘confluence’ + hyll ‘hill’) + tūn as the origin. This surname is also found in Scotland and Ireland.

History: A Scottish family of this name derives it from lands at Middleto(u)n near Kincardine. The Scottish physician Peter Middleton practiced in New York City after 1752 and was one of the founders of the medical school at King's College (now Columbia University) in 1767. One of the earliest of the Charleston, SC, Middleton family of prominent legislators was Arthur Middleton, born in Charleston in 1681. The name has also been established in Ireland since at least the 13th century; its presence was reinforced by settlers in the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: Berwick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name primarily from Berwick-on-Tweed, on the Northumbrian coast at the mouth of the Tweed river, a border town that regularly changed hands between the Scots and the English. Also from any of the places called Berwick in Dorset and elsewhere, Berrick in Oxfordshire, Barwick in Norfolk and Yorkshire, or Barricks in High Easter, Essex. This could also be a topographic name for someone who lived ‘(at the) outlying grange’, from Middle English ber(e)wik ‘outlying grange or farm, demesne farm’ (Old English berewīc ‘barley farm’).

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: On December 6, 1606, the journey to Virginia began on three ships: the Susan Constant, the Godspeed, and the Discovery. In 1607, 104 English men and boys arrived in North America to start a settlement. On May 13 they picked Jamestown, Virginia for their settlement, which was named after their King, James I. The settlement became the first permanent English settlement in North America... In 1698, fire struck Jamestown. The fire was evidently started by a prisoner awaiting execution in the nearby prison. The fire destroyed the prison and the statehouse, though many of the public records were saved. In 1699, the government and capital were moved from Jamestown to Middle Plantation, renamed Williamsburg. People continued to live on Jamestown Island and owned farm lands, but it ceased to be a town.
historicEvents.urlTitle: National Park Service - Historic Jamestowne - A Short History of Jamestown

historicEvents.description: The Virginia Company founded the first permanent English colony at Jamestown in 1607. One of the original 13 states (it entered the Union in 1788). Virginia holds an important place in American history, as it was home to many of the founding fathers, including George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, James Madison, George Mason, and Patrick Henry. Four of Americas first five presidents were Virginians. During the Civil War, Richmond, Virginias capital, was the capital of the Confederacy. Today, Virginia is a popular tourist spot where people can visit historic places such as Alexandria, Williamsburg, and Mount Vernon, George Washingtons estate.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Berwick Milton was born in 1570, in Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, England, his father, Richard Edison Melton II, was 34 and his mother, Elizabeth Houghton, was 35. He married Francisca Tayler on 5 August 1589, in Settrington, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. He died in 1640, in Jamestown, Virginia, British Colonial America, at the age of 70, and was buried in Jamestown, Virginia, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Berwick Milton est né en 1570, à Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Edison Melton II, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Houghton, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Francisca Tayler le 5 août 1589, à Settrington, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé en 1640, à Jamestown, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterré à Jamestown, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Berwick Milton 1570 in Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Edison Melton II 34 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Houghton 35. Er hat am 5. August 1589 in Settrington, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Francisca Tayler geheiratet. Er starb 1640 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Jamestown, Virginia, British Colonial America und wurde in Jamestown, Virginia, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Berwick Milton è nato il nel 1570 nel Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, England, il suo padre, Richard Edison Melton II, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Houghton, aveva 35 anni. Lui sposato Francisca Tayler il 5 agosto 1589, nel Settrington, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì nel 1640, nel Jamestown, Virginia, British Colonial America, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolto a Jamestown, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Berwick Miltonが1570年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアスタントン スト ジョンで生まれた時，父Richard Edison Melton IIは34歳，母Elizabeth Houghtonは35歳であった。1589年8月5日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアセットリントンでFrancisca Taylerと結婚した。彼は1640年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ジャムストウンで70歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ジャムストウンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Berwick Milton(이)가 1570년 영국 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 스트 존에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Edison Melton II(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Houghton(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1589년 8월 5일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 세트링턴에서 Francisca Tayler(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1640년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 자메스타운에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 자메스타운에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Berwick Milton nasceu em 1570, em Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Edison Melton II, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Houghton, tinha 35 anos. Ele casou-se com Francisca Tayler em 5 de agosto de 1589, em Settrington, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 1640, em Jamestown, Virginia, British Colonial America, com 70 anos, e foi sepultado em Jamestown, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Berwick Milton родился в 1570 г. в местности стантон ст джон, оксфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Edison Melton II было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Houghton, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Francisca Tayler 5 августа 1589 г. в местности сеттрингтон, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер в 1640 г. в местности джеместоун, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности джеместоун, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Berwick Milton nació en 1570, en Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Edison Melton II, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Houghton, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Francisca Tayler el 5 de agosto de 1589, en Settrington, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió en 1640, en Jamestown, Virginia, British Colonial America, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultado en Jamestown, Virginia, British Colonial America.
